subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is weak
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly discredits the West, specifically Americans, by using highly derogatory language, implying they are morally weak and reprehensible.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: time to stop caring about the people that do not care..they are the enemy of one sort or the other.. time they took responsibility (rather than consequences) of what their politicians are doing in their name
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement suggests that the West is indifferent to the plight of others, such as those in Ukraine, and is only concerned with its own interests, implying a lack of genuine care or concern for the well-being of Ukraine.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies a belief that diplomatic efforts are futile and that conflict, even to the point of nuclear war, is inevitable or preferable to ongoing diplomatic failures, directly discrediting the potential of diplomacy.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement presents a narrative that the West has consistently exaggerated or fabricated threats from Russia and China, which supports the parent narrative of discrediting the West but does not fit neatly into the other provided subnarratives.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Speculating war outcomes: Russian army is collapsing

subnarratives.reasoning: The text implies that the Russian army is not losing, contrary to Western media reports, which suggests that the Russian army is actually in a better position than reported.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: The text evokes fear and anxiety about the use of nuclear weapons, suggesting that the situation is so dire that even a nuclear war might be preferable to the current state of affairs, implying a real possibility of nuclear weapons being employed.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: By continuing the war we risk WWIII
subnarratives.evidence_quote: New Sarah Westall & Scott Ritter: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
subnarratives.reasoning: The title of the interview implies that the situation in Ukraine is serious and that the nuclear threat is a sign of desperation, which can be interpreted as a warning against continuing the war and risking a larger conflict, potentially WWIII.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: time to stop caring about the people that do not care..they are the enemy of one sort or the other.. time they took responsibility (rather than consequences) of what their politicians are doing in their name
